Updated: 9/15/2001
Original Dragon Ball airing at 5:00 and 5:30 pm on Cartoon Network
New episodes of DBZ currently airing on the Cartoon Network at 6:00 pm.
Movie #5 expected in January 2002, #6 to follow shortly thereafter. #5 will not be released theatrically, #6 unlikely.
Infogrames/Wizard Works is currently producing the upcoming DBZ games. First game is "The Legacy of Goku" for Game Boy Advance. Due before Christmas 2001.
 
Note: The following dates are provided by the manufactures. Some retailers may receive their shipments early.


DVD

Dragon Ball Z
Frieza: Desperation
(9/18/2001)
Frieza: Super Saiyan Goku
(10/16/2001)
Babidi: Descent
(10/2/2001)
Babidi: Battle Royal
(10/2/2001)
Babidi: The Dark Prince Returns
(10/30/2001)
Babidi: Rivals
(10/30/2001)


VHS

Dragon Ball
Tournament: Semi-Finals
(10/23/2001)
Tournament: The Final Test
(10/23/2001)

Dragon Ball Z
Babidi: The Dark Prince Returns
(9/25/2001)
Babidi: Rivals
(9/25/2001)


Comics

Dragon Ball
Graphic Novel: Vol. 6
(September 2001)
Part 4, #3
(10/5/2001)

Dragon Ball Z
Graphic Novel: Vol. 6
(October 2001)
Part 4, #11
(10/12/2001)

Saturday, 9/22/2001 @ 12:26 PM PDT by: Jon Allen
Man I'm bored.
For those who missed it, Dragonball's first season back on the air concluded with the finale of the first Tenkaichi Budokai. I have no idea when we'll be seeing new episodes, but they're currently in the process of being dubbed. There's still another little matter with the episodes they've already done that's kind of holding them back from working on new ones, but I can't comment on that just yet.

You may have noticed that the final hand to hand exchange with Jackie Chun and Gokou (after they've used up all of their power) was devoid of any background music. Unfortunately, a song titled "Mezase Tenkaichi" was supposed to have been playing during this scene, leading many to wonder if FUNimation's claim of using all of the original Japanese BGM was true. I'm going to go ahead and take a quick guess at the reason behind this. One of the main reasons that Dragonball Z was without the original back ground music was because, in DBZ, there were frequently songs in the series produced by third party companies ("Battle Power Infinity, Spirit Vs Spirit," etc.), complicating matters further to what was already a pricey purchase. To my knoledge, original Dragonball is less complicated in that respect, so they could have purchased the bgm without the few image songs. This is seriously a guess on my part, mainly because I'd hate to think they took the song out simply because it contained Japanese lyrics. They certainly had no problem playing "The World's Strongest" and "Dead Zone" with the two insert songs included (in original Japanese). I'll see what I can do to figure this one out.

On a completely different note, thanks to Kyle Hebert dropping by the forum, I got curious as to who else the fans might want to see grace the board. Go here and cast your vote.

That'll do Jon...that'll do.
Friday, 9/21/2001 @ 12:02 AM PDT by: Mr.E
Introducing our Newest Affiliate!
We are pleased to display a button link on the left-hand side of the page to our newest sponsor, ToyWiz.com. Among other things, they are selling both the Bandai and Irwin toys at their store. Check them out if you're interested in some cool imports (one of the few retail places that sells them online), or the new American figures.

Note: My e-mail is STILL down...sort of on purpose. I've been trying to get adjusted to the UCI environment (which is going well I might add), and sitting in front of the computer all day isn't going to cut it. So until then...DO NOT E-MAIL ME unless you are retarded and it's all you know how to do. I can't read what you send anyway. I'll let you guys know when things are back to normal.
Wednesday, 9/19/2001 @ 7:53 PM PDT by: Kaio
More Censorship, Bebop Returns!
Py86 sez:

I thought that maybe you could tell [Mr. E] that Cowboy Bebop is coming back October 4, 2001 at it's regural time. Since he posted about Bebop being canned I thought he'd be happy to know it returning. No information on whether it will air again after this, but I guess we'll find out on a day by day basis. If you follow the link it should give you proof Bebop is coming back.

Thanks.

For those who didn't realize it, there was a reasonable amount of sarcasm in my previous post. I hoped that the caps would emphasize that, but it's kind of difficult to convey sarcasm through text. Anyway, some more censorship news:

From chesspeaceface:
Just writing you a little bit of what I noticed while watching the DB series on CN as a follow up to your update. I noticed the digitally edited cow heads in the corners as well. If you watch closely throughout the series they've edited a lot of things in that way. In the episode where their boat breaks down, Oolong turns into a fish and runs away. Goku gets naked and swims after him then they try putting some bills on a fishing rod to catch him. While goku is standing there, they digitally penciled in some briefs on him as well as an American dollar pattern. On today's episode where he turns back from oozaru, there was a brick selectively placed in front of goku's "gear", digitally edited in also. If you look closely at these three examples, as well as the mad cow thing, the image bounces around alot. Just some more info about it.

From: DBZN Staff
They skipped "Gohan's First Date" today on CN to the
very next episode. Possibly because it contains
burning buildings/skyscrapers in it. I think that
means people won't be able to see that episode until
they re-run the saga after the buu saga. We posted
something about on our site dbzn.net. I just thought
it would be news of interest, and the more people that
know about it...the more likely we will get an
explanation from FUNi. Peace, Kaervak

From: Martin
Hi, well another major censoring is in the fight between Krillin and Bacterium. When Krillin slides under Bacterium, he passes gas and Krillen smells it and gets knocked out. When I was watching it on the CN the other day, when Krillin was under him, all of the sudden he fell on the ground. That's about 4 seconds that they cut out. Just to let ya know. Put me on the front page!!

There you have it! Just to dam the potential river of emails: please do not send me any censorship information unless it is in relevance to the episode aired on the day you send it. I'm really not interested in stuff from episodes past.
Wednesday, 9/19/2001 @ 12:32 PM PDT by: Kaio
FUNimation Censoring? NO WAY!
c4pt41n cr4x0rz (watch out, he'll hack you silly) reports:

Yesterday on the second episode of dragonball, in which Goku is fighting "Jackie Cheung", after his triple after-image attack and the hit on the head, Roshi goes into whats clearly a comically accidental Drunken Master technique. Sadly, its dubbed as follows:

Yamcha yells something to the effect of "Don't be fooled, he's using the Mad Cow technique!"  Goku: "Of course, my grandfather was a master of that."

They cut to a scene of Roshi's thoughts, showing two cows that look stylistically totally out of place. All the while, Roshi brings his hand up to his mouth, as if hes holding a bottle... the kids must be wondering what the deal is with THAT.

Of course, all in the name of protecting impressionable youth from the idea that alcohol consumption improves fighting ability. And in effect, they keep general knowledge from them.

There you have it. FUNimation is CENSORING Dragon Ball!
Tuesday, 9/18/2001 @ 9:42 PM PDT by: Jon Allen
Lookin' for love in all the wrong places...
That wacky Kyle Hebert has graced our humble little message board yet again, this time looking for more direct feedback on his work now that season 5 has begun airing on Toonami. Drop by this thread and let him know what you've thought of his work thus far, or just sit back and learn as he answers some questions. Be it good or bad, keep the feedback constructive, as he's very skittish. It's fun and easy, so get the whole family in on this! Mom, Dad, Sis, uh, no old people though, they're not covered by our insurance.

And remember, if Kyle's not smiling when you say his Gohan is great, your next update is free.
Monday, 9/17/2001 @ 5:11 PM PDT by: Mr.E
My E-mail is Busted...
Just to let you guys know...I'll keep you posted, but I currently can't access mail from Sunday onward. Thanks for your cooperation.
Monday, 9/17/2001 @ 1:00 AM PDT by: Jon Allen
Dragonball Eyecatchers/commercial bumpers on Toonami
Just a quick note here. I've unfortunately missed a number of Dragonball episodes lately, but apparently on an episode that aired last Thursday, "Elimination Round," both of the Japanese commercial bumpers were used. Very neato torpedo. One more bit of proof that the dubbing for original Dragonball has been done with the original fan's wishes in mind. The eyecatchers haven't been on the tapes, but will be on the DVDs, in case anyone was curious.

The news about the bumpers being on Toonami was reported to me by Brad Byers, and I'm going to take his word on this because he seems like a spiffy guy.
Sunday, 9/16/2001 @ 8:41 PM PDT by: Julian
Amidst it all, the Community still thrives...
Hello once again, ladies and gentlemen. I think it's safe to say that it's been a rather irregular week for all of us living in the US. However, I would prefer not to say much more about that. I don't want to be perceived as callous (I happen to have more than a few friends and relatives who work in NYC), but I think that nearly everything I could say has already been said, and much more eloquently than my writing skills allow. Perhaps the best piece on the matter that shares my point of view (except for the large age gap between the author and myself) was written by Miami Herald humorist Dave Barry, but his column is fairly long and would take up space that I need for my update. With that in mind, let us continue.

  • DB Japan has updated both its "Strange Items" and "Manga Scans" section, as well as its scans, screencaps, fanart, and fanfics.

  • DB Kenkon has gotten some new fanart and put its link descriptions back up.

  • Future Foretold now has 29 new screencaps from the Videl/Supopobitchi fight, and a new music video.

  • Ginga GIRI GIRI's Movies section is back up and running. In addition, for your perusal are 11 new clips: 9 from the live-action DB movie and 2 from the Buu saga.

  • Planet Z, who I apparently forgot to mention last week (though I thought I did, but maybe I didn't), has added some fanfic chapters. If I forgot to mention it, their server has changed locations, to the address provided in the link.

  • Saiyanz Rage has an interview with Pojo up, a new "dub voices" section detailing nearly every English voice for DB and DBZ, and a transcript of the interview with Eric Johnson conducted on the official site.

...And that's it for this week. Before I go, however, I'd like to take the time to relate to you a true story. It all started when Jon Allen posted his thoughts about the first Ano-yo'ichi Budoukai DBZ episode. I, being pretty well-versed in the ways of the dub, thought he had made a mistake by crediting Mark Britten with the role of Dai Kaiou. I IM-ed him, told him that he was wrong, and proceeded to prove my point... at which time I realized that I was the one who was wrong. He didn't seem too bothered by the whole thing, but it's not too good for the ol' ego, I gotta tell ya. The moral of this story: Don't f*** with Jon. Daizenshuu Jon was great. Let us all now take the time to pay homage to the almighty master of trivial knowledge about FUNimation. Or not.

'Til next week...
Sunday, 9/16/2001 @ 8:07 PM PDT by: Mr.E
Bebop CANNED...
Grrr...Mr.E smash! While I own the entire series on DVD, this still pisses me off to no end. And I bet it has something to do with the episode where a prison shipped is hijacked by one of the inmates. There's no way that Cowboy Bebop's ratings were that bad. NO WAY. Here's the word from Sam:

It's official: Cowboy Bebop is cancelled after only 4 episodes.

Two confirmation sources:

1) It's not on either Sundays or Thursdays on the schedule pages of Cartoon Network's web site.

2) When I clicked "view all showings" of Cowboy Bebop on September 9 (last week), there were no future episodes listed.

What a load of horseshit. Should we purge the media of anything terrorist-related because of actual events? NBC has been airing news about it continuously for four days, why can't we enjoy a fictional show along the same lines? Note: I'm not sure if this is the reason that Bebop was canceled, it just seems to be the greatest likelihood. Sigh.

Disclaimers
Planet Namek is just a fansite, we did NOT create Dragon Ball, nor did we have any part in the production process. There are some items on this website that may be questionable for those under 13 years of age. Dragon Ball was intended for young children in Japan who don't view such material as offensive.

©2001, Bird Studio/Shueisha, Toei Animation. Licensed by FUNimation Productions, Inc. All Rights Reserved, or as otherwise directed by FUNimation. This site is not meant to infringe on any copyrights, it is simply here to encourage people to watch the Dragon Ball series.

Although we did not create the series, we did take a lot of time to work on this site and bring you original files and information. Please honor our efforts by not stealing our files/layout from us. Thank you.

Web Page Design © Planet Namek

For the best experience, set your browser to view images "better" rather than "faster."
